title: Quiz: Quantum Computing Connection
description: Test your understanding of fermion-to-qubit mappings

questions.question: Why can't we simply map c†ᵢ → σ⁺ᵢ without modification?

options.text: σ⁺ operators on different qubits commute, but c† should anticommute

questions.explanation: The key issue is statistics: fermionic operators anticommute (c†₁c†₂ = -c†₂c†₁), but qubit operators on different sites commute (σ⁺₁σ⁺₂ = σ⁺₂σ⁺₁). Jordan-Wigner strings fix this.

questions.question: In Jordan-Wigner, what is c†₂ for a 4-qubit system?
questions.explanation: Jordan-Wigner requires Z operators on all qubits with index less than j. For c†₂: c†₂ = Z₀ ⊗ Z₁ ⊗ σ⁺₂ ⊗ I.

questions.question: How many qubits are needed for H₂ in a minimal (STO-3G) basis?
questions.explanation: H₂ in STO-3G has 2 spatial orbitals (σ and σ*). With spin, that's 4 spin orbitals, requiring 4 qubits (one per spin orbital).

questions.question: What is the main advantage of Bravyi-Kitaev over Jordan-Wigner?

options.text: Reduces Pauli string weight from O(N) to O(log N)

questions.explanation: Bravyi-Kitaev uses a binary tree encoding that reduces the average weight of Pauli strings from O(N) to O(log N), requiring fewer gates for simulation.

questions.question: The number operator n̂ⱼ = c†ⱼcⱼ maps to a single Z operator in Jordan-Wigner.
questions.explanation: True. n̂ⱼ = c†ⱼcⱼ = (I - Zⱼ)/2 in Jordan-Wigner. The Z strings cancel when c† and c are on the same site, leaving just a local Z operator.

questions.question: How many Pauli terms are typically in a molecular Hamiltonian?
questions.explanation: The number of Pauli terms scales as O(N⁴) due to the two-electron integrals. This dominates over the O(N²) one-electron terms.

questions.question: What is the qubit Hamiltonian after fermion-to-qubit mapping?

options.text: A sum of weighted Pauli strings

questions.explanation: The qubit Hamiltonian is expressed as H = Σₖ wₖ Pₖ, a weighted sum of Pauli strings. This form is used directly in VQE energy estimation.

questions.question: Which are valid fermion-to-qubit mappings? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Jordan-Wigner, Bravyi-Kitaev, and Parity are all fermion-to-qubit mappings. Hartree-Fock is a classical approximation method, not a qubit mapping.
